options.content:
# W&B Artifacts

Use this when a customer says **"my artifact won't upload / won't download / is slow / a version is missing."** This dashboard covers the user-facing artifact surface: uploading and downloading artifacts, versioning and collections, and the background jobs (garbage collection, TTL) that clean up artifact storage.

**How to work top to bottom:**
- **Artifact operations** — are artifact actions still happening, and how big/slow are they?
- **Errors** — are specific artifact create/add operations failing?
- **Garbage collection & TTL** — is background cleanup healthy? A GC backlog or failures make storage grow and can leave orphaned data.
- **Traces** — jump into an individual slow artifact request.

All application (DogStatsD) metrics are per-namespace; the trace panel is sampled. For the object store and datastore behind artifacts, see the [W&B Datastores](/d/wandb-datastores) dashboard; for API-level upload/download HTTP status see [W&B Field Investigation](/d/wandb-field-investigation).

panels.title: Artifact action rates
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). artifact_ArtifactActions / artifactCollection_ArtifactActions / artifactType_ArtifactActions are filtered {namespace="$namespace"}. The breakdown label is assumed to be `action`; if the legend shows blanks, run label_values(artifact_ArtifactActions) to find the real action-type label and adjust the `by (action)` clause.

options.content:
**What it shows** — the rate of artifact operations (create, add, read, delete, etc.) across artifacts, collections, and types.

**What bad looks like** — activity dropping to zero while the customer says they are actively pushing artifacts (nothing is landing), or one action type spiking abnormally.

**Do next** — if activity is flat-zero, the request may not be reaching the server at all: check upload/download HTTP status on [W&B Field Investigation](/d/wandb-field-investigation). If actions are happening but failing, see the **Errors** section below.

panels.title: Manifest entries per artifact (size)
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). manifest_dist_entries is a histogram — this uses manifest_dist_entries_sum / _count / _bucket, all filtered {namespace="$namespace"}.

options.content:
**What it shows** — how many files (manifest entries) each artifact contains, average and p95. This is the artifact's size in file count.

**What bad looks like** — a large jump in p95 entries lining up with the slowness the customer reports; very large artifacts (tens of thousands of files) are slow to upload, download, and manifest.

**Do next** — if a slow artifact is large, that is often the explanation. Confirm the operation duration in the next panel, and grab a trace via **Open Traces in Explore** (top-right).

panels.title: Fix-artifact-size job duration
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). fix_artifact_size_dist_duration is a histogram — this uses _sum / _count / _bucket, filtered {namespace="$namespace"}. Unit is assumed milliseconds; switch to seconds if the values look 1000x off.

options.content:
**What it shows** — how long the fix-artifact-size job takes to compute artifact sizes, average and p95.

**What bad looks like** — p95 duration climbing into many seconds, which usually tracks with very large artifacts (see the manifest-entries panel above).

**Do next** — if this is slow at the same time the customer sees slow artifacts, correlate with artifact size. This is a backend job, not the user's request path directly — for the actual request, open a trace via **Open Traces in Explore** (top-right).

panels.title: Artifact create / add errors
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). create_artifact_collection_not_found_after_fetch / create_artifact_invalid_client_id_digest / add_artifact_to_collection_collection_not_found_after_insert are filtered {namespace="$namespace"}.

options.content:
**What it shows** — specific failure counters in the artifact create and add-to-collection paths.

**What bad looks like** — any sustained non-zero rate. `invalid client id digest` points at the SDK/client sending bad data (often a version mismatch); `collection not found` after a fetch/insert points at a race or a consistency problem server-side.

**Do next** — for `invalid client id digest`, confirm the customer's SDK version (see Service Image Versions on [W&B Field Investigation](/d/wandb-field-investigation)). For the `collection not found` counters, snapshot this panel and **escalate** with the namespace and time range.

panels.title: Garbage collection outcomes
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). artifacts_garbage_collect_success / _failure / _deleted_files / _missing_manifest and artifact_builder_safe_to_delete_files are filtered {namespace="$namespace"}.

options.content:
**What it shows** — the artifact garbage-collector's results: successes vs failures, files it deleted, and `missing manifest` events.

**What bad looks like** — `gc failure` sustained non-zero, or `success` dropping to zero (GC stalled) while `safe-to-delete files` keeps climbing. GC failure or backlog means storage grows without bound. `missing manifest` means GC found data whose manifest is gone — potential orphaned data.

**Do next** — snapshot this panel and **escalate** if failures or `missing manifest` are firing; note the namespace and time range. Check object-store capacity on [W&B Datastores](/d/wandb-datastores).

panels.title: TTL deletions & expansion errors
panels.description: TODO: DogStatsD metric — currently EMPTY because gorilla runs with GORILLA_STATSD_PORT=0, which disables DogStatsD emission (a core/manifest-side setting, not the operator); it populates once that port is set. Names may carry a service prefix; if still empty after enabling, check label_values(__name__). artifact_ttl_deletion / artifact_entity_ttl_expansion_error are filtered {namespace="$namespace"}.

options.content:
**What it shows** — artifacts deleted by TTL policy, and errors while expanding entity-level TTL rules.

**What bad looks like** — a spike in `ttl deletions` if a customer reports an artifact vanished unexpectedly (check whether a TTL policy caught it), or any sustained `entity ttl expansion error` (TTL rules are not being applied correctly).

**Do next** — if a customer says data disappeared, correlate the deletion spike with their report and confirm the intended TTL policy. For expansion errors, snapshot and **escalate** with the namespace and time range.

panels.title: Artifact operation latency p95 (traces, sampled)
panels.description: Trace-derived (traces_spanmetrics_*, confirmed present) so it is SAMPLED — trust the shape, not exact values. The span_name regex matches any span containing 'artifact'; if this panel is empty, run label_values(traces_spanmetrics_duration_milliseconds_bucket, span_name) and adjust the regex to the artifact span names on your stack.

options.content:
**What it shows** — p95 latency of artifact-related spans from sampled traces, split by span. A quick way to see which artifact operation is slow.

**What bad looks like** — one artifact span's p95 climbing into seconds.

**Do next** — to see an individual slow artifact request end to end, use the **Open Traces in Explore** link (top-right), set the service to gorilla and filter for `duration > 3s` and an artifact operation, then read the span chain. If this panel is empty, adjust the span-name regex (see the panel description).
